Acetosella is a genus of plants in the family Oxalidaceae, commonly known as wood sorrels. These plants are known for their edible leaves and stems, which have been used in traditional medicine for their health benefits.

The health benefits of Acetosella plants include their potential to act as a diuretic, to help reduce fever, and to aid in digestion. The bioactive constituents of these plants include oxalates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

The most common herbal preparations made with Acetosella plants include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support urinary health and to alleviate digestive issues.
